assessment.title: Session 4 Quiz – Arrays and Array Methods

material.mattext:
Session 4 Quiz – Arrays and Array Methods

Test your understanding of arrays, array manipulation, and higher-order array methods.

Topics Covered: - Array creation and access - push, pop, shift, unshift - map, filter, reduce - forEach, find, findIndex - Array destructuring - Spread operator

Instructions: - 16 questions from the Session 4 question bank - 20 minute time limit - 3 attempts allowed - 16 points total (1 point per question)

After syncing the question bank to Canvas:
What kind of loop should you use when you don't know in advance how many times to repeat?
Loops always require a known count
What will this print? ```js let count = 3; while (count > 0) { console.log(count); count--; } ```
Which loop correctly iterates over every element in an array without needing an index?
What will this print? ```js const fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]; for (const fruit of fruits) { console.log(fruit); } ```
What is the risk of a while loop that never changes its condition?
What does this pattern produce? ```js const prices = [10, 20, 30]; const doubled = []; for (const price of prices) { doubled.push(price * 2); } console.log(doubled); ```
When should you use `for...of` instead of a classic `for` loop?
When you need the element's index
When you only need each element's value
What does this build-new-array pattern require before the loop? ```js const result = []; for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { result.push(nums[i] * 2); } ```
An empty array declared before the loop starts
The loop variable must start at 1
What does this for loop do? ```js for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { console.log(i); } ```
The loop variable that tracks the current iteration
What will this print? ```js const items = [10, 20, 30]; for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) { console.log(items[i]); } ```
What does `i++` do in a for loop?
Adds 1 to i after each iteration
How many times does this loop run? ```js for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) { console.log("Hi"); } ```
What will this code print? ```js const scores = [85, 92, 78]; for (let i = 0; i < scores.length; i++) { console.log(scores[i]); } ```
What is the purpose of a loop?
In a loop, what does `scores.length` give you?
The first item in the array
The number of items in the array
What will this code output? ```js for (let i = 1; i <= 3; i++) { console.log(i * 2); } ```
How would you loop through an array backwards?
Just use a regular for loop
Start i at the end and decrement (i--)
Use a special backwards loop keyword
What happens if your loop condition is always true?
When you need to repeat code for multiple items
What will this print? ```js const prices = [5, 10, 15]; let sum = 0; for (let i = 0; i < prices.length; i++) { sum = sum + prices[i]; } console.log(sum); ```
It ensures you loop through every item without going past
Can you modify array elements inside a loop?
Yes, you can access and modify them
